structural_claim: Information about sources can be preserved through complex scattering processes despite apparent information loss or scrambling

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Black holes appear to erase information, reducing all matter to three parameters, yet the information paradox suggests it must be preserved
Information in gravitational systems is holographically encoded on boundaries, preserving it despite bulk dynamics
Wave propagation through complex scattering media preserves source information enabling perfect time-reversal refocusing
